pattern: The sequence describes its own run lengths, so you grow it by reading earlier terms as the counts for the next runs, alternating the symbol.
hints: You never need to store anything but the sequence itself — each value already tells you how long the next run of the alternating symbol should be.
Use one pointer into the part you have already built; the value it points at is how many copies of the current symbol to append next, then flip the symbol.
Seed the sequence, then while it is shorter than n keep appending runs whose lengths come from the read pointer; finally count how many of the first n entries equal 1.

edge_inputs.description: first six elements contain three ones
moderate n where naive run-length bookkeeping is easy to get off by one

stress.description: large n confirms the self-referential generation stays consistent
